{{Quote|Having to work behind the scenes hasn't destroyed the magic, but it has broken the illusion of the ''Star Wars'' universe. But it's also invigorating to have a hand in the process of creation. It's a universe that has a life of its ownnow.|Peet Janes|<ref name="gathering" />}}
'''Peet Janes''' was an editor at [[Dark Horse Comics]] and the main editor of Dark Horse's ''[[Star Wars]]'' line from the mid-'90s to 2001.

Janes grew up in Rhode Island and in his youth he was reading the [[Star Wars (comic strip)|''Star Wars'' strip]] as published by the ''Newport Daily News''.<ref name="gathering">''[[The Secrets of Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'', Part 5: Shadow Play, I. GATHERING OF THE TRIBES</ref>

Janes was in charge of numerous titles as the Dark Horse line expanded and flourished, such as [[Star Wars: X-Wing Rogue Squadron|'' X-Wing: Rogue Squadron'']], [[Star Wars: Droids: The Adventures of R2-D2 and C-3PO|''Star Wars Droids'']], ''[[Classic Star Wars]]'', ''[[Classic Star Wars: The Early Adventures]]'', ''[[Star Wars: Mara Jade – By the Emperor's Hand]]'', ''[[Boba Fett: Enemy of the Empire]]'', ''[[Star Wars: Vader's Quest]]'', ''[[The Jabba Tape]]'', ''[[Shadow Stalker]]'', ''[[The Bounty Hunters: Aurra Sing]]'', ''[[The Bounty Hunters: Kenix Kil]]'', ''[[Boba Fett: Twin Engines of Destruction]]'', ''[[Star Wars: Union]]'', [[Star Wars: Chewbacca (Dark Horse)|''Star Wars: Chewbacca'']], and ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]''. He was also the first editor of the ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' anthology series, as well as the writer of Dark Horse's ''[[Star Wars Handbook]]'', a series of encyclopedic guides to the existing Dark Horse ''Star Wars'' titles.

Editor through the release of the film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], Janes oversaw the transition from post–[[original trilogy]]/[[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]]–inspired content to the [[Prequel trilogy|prequel]]-influenced content at Dark Horse. He edited the Episode I spin-off comics ''[[Episode I: Obi-Wan Kenobi]]'', ''[[Episode I: Queen Amidala]]'', and ''[[Episode I: Qui-Gon Jinn]]''.

In addition, Janes worked on Dark Horse's first comics adaptation of ''Star Wars'' novels, including ''[[The Last Command]]'' (co-edited with Bob Cooper), ''[[Splinter of the Mind's Eye]]'', and ''[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire]]'', Lucas Licensing's first cross-media ''Star Wars'' comics/novel/video-game event.

He was preceded by editor [[Bob Cooper]] and succeeded by [[David Land|Dave Land]].
